description: Proactive On-Premise Database Monitoring Dashboard for Oracle & MySQL

panels.description: Database instance availability status. Green = UP, Red = DOWN
panels.title: Instance Status

panels.description: Replication lag in seconds. Warning > 30s, Critical > 5m

targets.expr:
# MySQL Replication Lag
mysql_slave_status_seconds_behind_master{instance=~"$instance.*"}
or
# Oracle Data Guard Lag (if available)
oracledb_dataguard_lag_seconds{instance=~"$instance.*"}
or
# Fallback: Return 0 if no replication configured
vector(0)

panels.title: Replication Lag

panels.description: Time since last successful backup. Critical if > 24 hours

targets.expr:
# Time since last backup (requires custom metric or RMAN exporter)
time() - (oracledb_backup_last_successful_timestamp{instance=~"$instance.*"} or mysql_backup_last_successful_timestamp{instance=~"$instance.*"} or vector(0))

panels.title: Backup Freshness

panels.description: Predictive: Days until disk is 100% full based on 24h growth rate. Warning < 14 days, Critical < 3 days
panels.title: Disk Time-to-Full (Predictive)

panels.description: CPU Load Average divided by CPU cores. Value > 1.0 indicates saturation.

targets.expr:
# CPU Load vs Cores ratio (1-minute load average / CPU count)
node_load1{instance=~"$instance.*"}
/
count without(cpu, mode) (node_cpu_seconds_total{instance=~"$instance.*", mode="idle"})

panels.title: CPU Load vs Cores

panels.description: Memory breakdown: Cached/Buffers (Good) vs Active/Application (Actual usage)

targets.expr:
# Cached Memory (Good usage for file caching)
node_memory_Cached_bytes{instance=~"$instance.*"}

panels.title: Memory Usage Breakdown

panels.description: Query latency percentiles: p50, p95, p99 to detect outliers and performance issues

targets.expr:
# MySQL Query Latency p50
histogram_quantile(0.50, rate(mysql_global_status_query_response_time_seconds_bucket{instance=~"$instance.*"}[$interval]))
or
# Oracle Response Time p50 (from wait events)
histogram_quantile(0.50, rate(oracledb_wait_time_seconds_bucket{instance=~"$instance.*"}[$interval]))

# MySQL Query Latency p95
histogram_quantile(0.95, rate(mysql_global_status_query_response_time_seconds_bucket{instance=~"$instance.*"}[$interval]))
or
# Oracle Response Time p95
histogram_quantile(0.95, rate(oracledb_wait_time_seconds_bucket{instance=~"$instance.*"}[$interval]))

# MySQL Query Latency p99
histogram_quantile(0.99, rate(mysql_global_status_query_response_time_seconds_bucket{instance=~"$instance.*"}[$interval]))
or
# Oracle Response Time p99
histogram_quantile(0.99, rate(oracledb_wait_time_seconds_bucket{instance=~"$instance.*"}[$interval]))

panels.title: Query Latency (Percentiles)

targets.expr:
# Oracle Transactions Per Second (TPS) - User Commits
rate(oracledb_activity_user_commits_total{instance=~"$instance.*"}[$interval])

panels.description: Deadlocks, lock waits, and aborted connections per second
panels.title: Deadlock / Error Rate

panels.description: MySQL user accounts status, password expiry, and security compliance

targets.rawSql:
SELECT 
    CONCAT(User, '@', Host) AS User,
    CASE 
        WHEN account_locked = 'Y' THEN 'Locked'
        WHEN password_expired = 'Y' THEN 'Expired'
        ELSE 'Open'
    END AS Status,
    COALESCE(password_last_changed, 'Never') AS Last_Password_Change,
    CASE 
        WHEN password_lifetime IS NOT NULL AND password_last_changed IS NOT NULL THEN 
            GREATEST(0, password_lifetime - DATEDIFF(NOW(), password_last_changed))
        WHEN password_lifetime IS NULL AND (SELECT @@default_password_lifetime) > 0 AND password_last_changed IS NOT NULL THEN
            GREATEST(0, (SELECT @@default_password_lifetime) - DATEDIFF(NOW(), password_last_changed))
        ELSE NULL
    END AS Days_Until_Expiry,
    account_locked AS Is_Locked,
    password_expired AS Is_Expired
FROM mysql.user
WHERE User NOT IN ('mysql.sys', 'mysql.session', 'mysql.infoschema', 'root')
ORDER BY Days_Until_Expiry ASC, User;

panels.title: MySQL User Security Status

panels.description: Oracle database session and user metrics from oracle_exporter. Note: For full user security audit, configure a direct Oracle datasource.
panels.title: Oracle Session Status

title: Database Proactive Monitoring
